title: salix pulchra subsp. parallelinervis
intro: Salix pulchra subsp. parallelinervis, also known as the parallelinervis willow, is a subspecies of Salix pulchra, a type of willow tree native to Alaska. The parallelinervis willow has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. The bark and leaves of the parallelinervis willow can be used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat various health issues.
